title: Mellow Sweet Dutch Endive Gratin
description: A rich and velvety solo dinner dish featuring Dutch endive, ground sugar, and namul blanch seasoning, served in a scallop shell, suitable for shojin friendly diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the Dutch endive and remove the tough outer leaves.
Blanch the endive in boiling water for 5 minutes, then immediately submerge in an ice bath.
In a small bowl, mix together the ground sugar, namul blanch seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the blanched endive to the skillet and sprinkle with the sugar and seasoning mixture.
Arrange the endive in a scallop shell, spooning some of the garlic and oil mixture over the top.
If using cheese, sprinkle it over the endive.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the endive is tender and the top is lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
